title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Dirty VLCC US tonne days to the Far East
Increase in the tonne days for VLCC-US dirty shipments from the USG to the Far East at the end of the year
In the second week of December, the outlook for the crude oil freight market is subdued despite earlier expectations of stronger momentum. In the clean segment, however, there are encouraging signs that sentiment is brightening, particularly on the LR2 MEG-Japan route. Amidst global geopolitical challenges related to Russian crude trade and the ongoing conflict between Israeland Hamas, it's notable that US crude exports have peaked in 2023.

children.title: Chart of the Week: Dirty VLCC US tonne days to the Far East
section_path: Chart of the Week: Dirty VLCC US tonne days to the Far East
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: Dirty VLCC US tonne days to the Far East
children.summary:
Increase in the tonne days for VLCC-US dirty shipments from the USG to the Far East at the end of the year
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T141437.747.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t141437747_3880e79b4611.png
In the second week of December, the outlook for the crude oil freight market is subdued despite earlier expectations of stronger momentum. In the clean segment, however, there are encouraging signs that sentiment is brightening, particularly on the LR2 MEG-Japan route.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
Market Rates (WS)
‘Dirty' WS - Weaker VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T141437.747.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t141437747_3880e79b4611.png
Freight rates for crude oil saw a significant decline, which was particularly noticeable in the Aframax-Med route, which showed a clear downward trend compared to the peak observed in week 44. VLCC MEG-China freight rates fell to 57 WS, 13% lower than in the previous week, when rates were still at 70 WS at the beginning of November.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR2 Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T141636.866.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t141636866_95fe031083d7.png
LR2 AG freight rates were around 145 WS, 10 points higher than in the previous week and 11% higher than in a comparable week in the previous month.

children.title: Panamax Steady
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T141808.283.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t141808283_c2e0a3702100.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates dropped below WS200, for the first time since mid-October, 60% weaker than a year ago.

children.title: ‘Clean' MR Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T141854.794.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t141854794_2bfe51a1b23a.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent revised downward at levels below 300 WS, a 43% increase compared to a similar week a year ago. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the US remained unchanged from the previous week at 195 WS, while MR2 USG-Cont rates fell 30 points from the previous week to around 248 WS, a 30% increase from a similar week a month ago.

children.title: 'Dirty' (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T143727.868.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t143727868_73929b515afc.png
In the second week of December, there is a weekly decline in the number of vessels on the above routes, with the VLCC Ras Tanura showing a downward correction below the annual average. VLCC Ras Tanura: The number of ships dropped to 54, which is 12 lower than the annual average, but still higher than the bottom of 47 seen during the week 45.

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T142834.145.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t142834145_54aafc6d96f9.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: There was a clear downward trend to 10, 8 lower than the high in week 45. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: Volatility remains high and the last four weeks have shown a mixed picture, while the latest activity indicated a drop in the number of vessels to 31, 8 less than three weeks ago.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T143005.335.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t143005335_5d21cd8bd225.png
Dirty tonne days: The downtrend correction in the Aframax segment is more pronounced, as the high of week 48 has now been corrected to weaker growth. In the VLCC segment, on the other hand, growth rates are following a weaker trend but are well above the recent low of week 44.

children.title: ‘Clean' Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-12-15T143115.621.png | assets/2023-12-15_3uqrykb5wetu5cvv76woxcc2gd599q_img_unnamed-2023-12-15t143115621_5caafbb8cfe9.png
Panamax tonne days: There seems to be a gradual upward revision following the downward correction at the end of week 46. Clean MR tonne days: The decline continued in both MR1 and MR2 vessel sizes, with growth rates now at their weakest after the recent peaks in week 42.
